questions.question: Select ALL important considerations in altered mental status due to acute hyponatremia.
choices: Consider hypertonic saline when severe neurological symptoms are present
Do not correct at an excessively rapid rate
Reassess sodium levels frequently
In chronic hyponatremia, be cautious of osmotic demyelination syndrome
Correct sodium rapidly all the way to the upper limit of normal at once
questions.explanation: Sodium correction in hyponatremia requires balancing prompt intervention for severe symptoms against avoidance of overcorrection. In chronic cases, osmotic demyelination syndrome must be prevented.

questions.question: Select THREE appropriate approaches to treatment and evaluation of hepatic encephalopathy.
choices: Search for precipitating factors such as gastrointestinal bleeding and infection
Consider lactulose
Evaluate for constipation and dehydration
Continually increase sedative medications without reassessment
Airway assessment is unnecessary even with altered mental status
questions.explanation: In hepatic encephalopathy, identify and eliminate precipitating factors, initiate treatment such as lactulose, and manage bowel habits and hydration. Airway protection and aspiration risk should also be assessed.

questions.question: Select ALL clinical clues that suggest autoimmune encephalitis.
choices: Acute to subacute psychiatric symptoms or memory impairment
Seizures
Involuntary movements or autonomic symptoms
CSF, MRI, or EEG (electroencephalogram) abnormalities
Simple orthostatic lightheadedness that fully resolves within minutes
questions.explanation: Autoimmune encephalitis is suspected when psychiatric symptoms, memory impairment, seizures, involuntary movements, and autonomic symptoms are present; differentiate from infectious encephalitis and connect the patient to specialist care.

questions.question: Select ALL appropriate management strategies for ICU delirium.
choices: Address pain, sleep, and the environment
Encourage early mobilization and maintain a day-night rhythm
Evaluate for causative medications, infection, and hypoxia
Consider orientation support using family presence, glasses, and hearing aids
Maintain deep sedation for an extended period in all patients
questions.explanation: Non-pharmacological interventions and identification of precipitating factors are fundamental to managing delirium. Prolonged deep sedation worsens delirium and delays mobilization and should be avoided.

questions.question: Select ALL situations in which continuous EEG monitoring should be considered.
choices: Altered mental status that persists after cessation of seizures
Unexplained persistent altered mental status
Suspected NCSE (non-convulsive status epilepticus)
Desire to evaluate the effect of antiepileptic drug treatment
Fully alert patient with no neurological symptoms, mild illness
questions.explanation: Continuous EEG monitoring is useful for diagnosing NCSE and assessing treatment response. It should be considered when seizure activity cannot be excluded based on clinical symptoms alone.
